title: rosa gallica f. pseudolivescens
intro: Rosa gallica f. pseudolivescens is a cultivar of the Gallic rose, a type of wild rose native to southern Europe and western Asia. This fragrant, pink-flowering plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Rosa gallica f. pseudolivescens include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and provide relief from menstrual cramps. Rosa gallica f. pseudolivescens can be prepared as a tea or tincture for herbal use, and its flowers and leaves can also be used to make a fragrant and medicinal infused oil.
